docs.title: Deepworks Core GitHub Repository
docs.text: This repository contains a collection of reusable GitHub Actions workflows. These workflows are designed to standardize common CI/CD tasks across Deepworks repositories.

docs.title: MkDocs GitHub Pages Workflow
docs.text:
View Documentation

Automates the deployment of MkDocs documentation to GitHub Pages. This workflow:

  • Copies repository files (README, CHANGELOG, LICENSE) to documentation
  • Updates MkDocs navigation configuration
  • Deploys to GitHub Pages
  • Supports custom Python versions and file locations

docs.title: Create GitHub Release
docs.text:
View Documentation

Automates the creation of GitHub releases. This workflow:

  • Creates releases from version tags
  • Supports prereleases and draft releases
  • Allows custom release titles
  • Can be triggered manually or by tags

docs.title: Update Changelog
docs.text:
View Documentation

Automatically updates CHANGELOG.md when pull requests are merged. This workflow:

  • Adds PR information to changelog
  • Manages unreleased section
  • Maintains consistent changelog format
  • Runs on PR merge to develop branch

docs.title: Using These Workflows
docs.text:
To use these workflows in your repository:

  1. Reference them in your workflow files:

    jobs:
docs:
    uses: deepworks-net/github.actions/.github/workflows/mkdocs-gh-pages.yml@main

  2. Configure with input parameters:

    with:
parameter: value

  3. Provide any required secrets:

    secrets:
token: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}

docs.title: Best Practices
docs.text:
When using these workflows:

  1. Always reference a specific tag/SHA instead of main for production use
  2. Test workflow changes in a feature branch first
  3. Monitor workflow runs for any issues
  4. Keep repository settings up to date with required permissions

docs.title: Need Help?
docs.text:
• Check individual workflow documentation for detailed configuration options
  • Review workflow run logs for troubleshooting
  • Open an issue if you encounter any problems
  • Submit pull requests for improvements

docs.title: Contributing
docs.text:
We welcome contributions! Please:

  1. Fork the repository
  2. Create a feature branch
  3. Make your changes
  4. Submit a pull request

See our Contributing Guidelines for more information.

docs.title: Getting Started with Deepworks Actions
docs.text: This guide will help you understand and implement Deepworks GitHub Actions workflows in your repositories.

docs.title: Overview
docs.text:
Deepworks Actions provide a suite of reusable workflows for:

  • Documentation management with MkDocs
  • Release management and versioning
  • Changelog automation
  • Repository standardization

docs.title: MkDocs GitHub Pages
docs.text:
Handles documentation deployment:

name: Documentation
jobs:
  docs:
    uses: deepworks-net/github.actions/.github/workflows/mkdocs-gh-pages.yml@main

docs.title: Release Management
docs.text:
Manages releases and versioning:

name: Release
jobs:
  release:
    uses: deepworks-net/github.actions/.github/workflows/release-drafter.yml@main

docs.title: Changelog Updates
docs.text:
Automates changelog maintenance:

name: Changelog
jobs:
  changelog:
    uses: deepworks-net/github.actions/.github/workflows/update-changelog.yml@main

docs.title: Workflow Integration Map
docs.text:
graph TD
    A[New PR] --> B{Merged to Develop}
    B --> C[Update Changelog]
    B --> D[Update Draft Release]
    C --> E[Prepare Release]
    D --> E
    E --> F[Create Release PR]
    F --> G[Merge to Main]
    G --> H[Publish Release]

docs.title: Setup Instructions
docs.text:
1. Repository Configuration
# .github/workflows/docs.yml
name: Documentation
on:
  push:
    branches:
      - main

jobs:
  docs:
    uses: deepworks-net/github.actions/.github/workflows/mkdocs-gh-pages.yml@main
  1. Required Files
  2. mkdocs.yml for documentation
  3. CHANGELOG.md for release notes

  4. .github/release-drafter.yml for release configuration

  5. Repository Settings

  6. Enable GitHub Pages
  7. Set appropriate branch protections
  8. Configure required status checks

docs.title: Documentation Updates
docs.text:
1. Make changes to docs
  2. Push to main
  3. Workflow automatically deploys

docs.title: Release Process
docs.text:
1. Create changes in feature branch
  2. Merge PR to develop
  3. Changelog updates automatically
  4. Draft release updates
  5. Prepare release when ready
  6. Merge release PR
  7. Final release publishes

docs.title: Custom MkDocs Settings
docs.text:
jobs:
  docs:
    uses: deepworks-net/github.actions/.github/workflows/mkdocs-gh-pages.yml@main
    with:
      python-version: '3.11'
      requirements-file: 'docs/requirements.txt'

docs.title: Release Configuration
docs.text:
jobs:
  release:
    uses: deepworks-net/github.actions/.github/workflows/release-drafter.yml@main
    with:
      tag-name: 'v1.0.0'

docs.title: Common Issues
docs.text:
1. Documentation Not Deploying
  2. Check GitHub Pages settings
  3. Verify mkdocs.yml configuration

  4. Review workflow permissions

  5. Release Draft Not Updating

  6. Check release-drafter.yml configuration
  7. Verify branch names

  8. Review GitHub token permissions

  9. Changelog Not Updating

  10. Ensure PR merged to correct branch
  11. Check file paths
  12. Verify workflow permissions

docs.text:
• Open an issue on GitHub

docs.title: Meta-Level Documentation: Git Development and Deployment Workflows
Overview
docs.text: This document provides a meta-level analysis of the Git-based development and deployment workflows used in the repository. The goal is to align these workflows conceptually and technically while identifying gaps and ensuring consistency.

docs.title: Purpose
docs.text: • Supports feature development, bug fixes, and other contributions using a branch-based workflow.

docs.title: Key Components
docs.text:
• Branches:
  • main: Stable release branch.
  • staging: Active development branch for integration testing.
  • Feature branches: For specific tasks or issues.
  • Commits: Follow conventional commit messages to ensure clarity.
  • Pull Requests (PRs): Used to merge feature branches into staging.

docs.title: Tools and Actions
docs.text:
• Git CLI for branch management.
  • Automated checks triggered by PR events (e.g., linting, testing).

docs.title: Known Issues
docs.text:
• Need for clearer branch naming conventions.
  • Standardization of commit message format.

docs.title: Gaps and Recommendations
docs.text:
• Document branch naming conventions explicitly.
  • Integrate a commit linter to enforce standards.

docs.title: Purpose
docs.text: • Manages the preparation, validation, and deployment of new releases.

docs.title: Key Components
docs.text:
• Tagging:
  • Pre-release tags: prep-vX.Y.Z to trigger workflows.
  • Final release tags: vX.Y.Z for stable releases.
  • Changelog Updates: Automated updates during release preparation.
  • Release Branches: Created during the preparation of major or minor versions.

docs.title: Tools and Actions
docs.text:
• Release Drafter: Generates draft release notes based on merged PRs.
  • Custom Actions:
  • version_calculation.py for semantic versioning.
  • update_changelog.py for changelog management.

docs.title: Known Issues
docs.text:
• Inconsistent changelog updates during pre-release workflows.
  • Potential gaps in tagging validation.

docs.title: Gaps and Recommendations
docs.text:
• Align release draft workflow with GitHub’s generation process.
  • Automate validation of tags and ensure consistency with semantic versioning.

docs.title: Purpose
docs.text: • Builds and deploys the documentation site via GitHub Pages.

docs.title: Key Components
docs.text:
• Pages Workflow: Automatically deploys changes from main to the GitHub Pages site.
  • MkDocs: Static site generator for documentation.

docs.title: Tools and Actions
docs.text:
• GitHub Actions:
  • mkdocs-gh-pages.yml for deployment.
  • Dependencies:
  • Listed in requirements.txt.

docs.title: Known Issues
docs.text:
• Occasional version mismatches between deployed site and repository source.
  • Lack of validation for MkDocs version consistency.

docs.title: Gaps and Recommendations
docs.text:
• Create a GitHub Action to validate MkDocs version consistency (back burner).
  • Add deployment logs for improved troubleshooting.

docs.title: Testing Strategy
docs.text:
To ensure the reliability of these workflows:

  1. Unit Tests for Custom Actions:
    • Validate scripts like version_calculation.py and update_changelog.py.
  2. Integration Tests:
    • Simulate end-to-end workflows to confirm alignment.
  3. Manual QA:
    • Periodically review Pages deployments for accuracy.

docs.title: Documentation Goals
docs.text:
• Create detailed guides for each workflow in the docs/ directory.
  • Ensure README and MkDocs documentation align with actual practices.
  • Maintain an up-to-date changelog that reflects all workflow updates.

docs.title: Next Steps
docs.text:
1. Audit Existing Workflows: Review YAML files and custom scripts for inconsistencies.
  2. Refine Documentation: Ensure all workflows are documented clearly and concisely.
  3. Test and Validate: Execute workflows to identify and fix gaps.
  4. Abstract and Generalize: Simplify changelog and versioning processes for flexibility.

docs.title: Conclusion
docs.text: By documenting, testing, and refining these workflows, the repository can achieve consistency, clarity, and alignment across development and deployment processes. These steps will help stabilize the repository and move toward the 0.1 milestone.

docs.title: Release Process Guide
docs.text: This guide details the complete release process used across Deepworks repositories.

docs.title: 1. Development Phase
docs.text:
graph TD
    A[Feature Branch] --> B[Create PR]
    B --> C[Merge to Develop]
    C --> D[Update Changelog]
    C --> E[Update Draft Release]
    D --> F[Next Development]
    E --> F

docs.title: Activities
docs.text:
• Feature development in branches
  • PRs created for review
  • Merges trigger automatic updates
  • Changelog entries added
  • Draft release updated

docs.title: 2. Release Preparation
docs.text:
graph TD
    A[Develop Branch] --> B[Create prep-v* Tag]
    B --> C[Create Release Branch]
    C --> D[Update Version Numbers]
    D --> E[Create Release PR]
    E --> F[Review Changes]
    F --> G[Merge to Develop]

docs.title: Process
docs.text:
1. Create prep tag:
git tag prep-v1.0.0
git push origin prep-v1.0.0
  1. Automated actions:
  2. Release branch created
  3. Changelog updated
  4. Version numbers updated
  5. Release PR created

docs.title: 3. Release Publication
docs.text:
graph TD
    A[Merged Release PR] --> B[Create Version Tag]
    B --> C[Publish Release]
    C --> D[Update Documentation]
    D --> E[Close Release Cycle]

docs.title: Automatic Version Incrementing
docs.text:
• Based on semantic versioning
  • Patch version = previous patch + commit count
  • Example:
Current: v1.0.16
3 new commits
Next: v1.0.19

docs.title: Version Tag Format
docs.text:
• Release tags: v1.0.0
  • Prep tags: prep-v1.0.0
  • Development: calculated automatically

docs.title: Structure
docs.text:
# Changelog

## **[(01/03/2025) - v1.0.16](link)**
- PR #45: Feature addition
- PR #44: Bug fix

## **01/03/2025 - Unreleased**
- PR #46: New feature

docs.title: Automation
docs.text:
1. PR merges add entries
  2. Release prep converts format
  3. Links added automatically
  4. Dates managed by workflow

docs.title: Draft Releases
docs.text:
• Updated automatically
  • Track ongoing changes
  • Version calculated dynamically
  • No manual intervention needed

docs.title: Prep Releases
docs.text:
• Created via prep tags
  • Convert changelog format
  • Create release branches
  • Generate release PRs

docs.title: Final Releases
docs.text:
• Published via version tags
  • Include all changes
  • Link to changelog
  • Permanent versions

docs.title: Branch Flow
docs.text:
graph LR
    A[Feature] --> B[Develop]
    B --> C[Release Branch]
    C --> D[Develop]
    D --> E[Main/Tag]

docs.title: Branch Naming
docs.text:
• Feature: feature/*
  • Release: release/v*
  • Main: main
  • Develop: develop

docs.title: Regular Development
docs.text:
1. Create feature branch
  2. Make changes
  3. Create PR
  4. Merge to develop
  5. Automatic updates occur

docs.title: Release Creation
docs.text:
1. Create prep tag
  2. Review release PR
  3. Merge release PR
  4. Create version tag
  5. Release publishes

docs.title: Hotfix Process
docs.text:
1. Branch from main
  2. Fix issue
  3. Create PR
  4. Special version handling
  5. Release creation

docs.title: Version Issues
docs.text:
• Check tag format
  • Verify commit history
  • Review version calculation

docs.title: Release Problems
docs.text:
• Check workflow permissions
  • Verify branch names
  • Review workflow logs

docs.title: Changelog Issues
docs.text:
• Check file format
  • Verify PR process
  • Review automation logs

docs.title: Best Practices
docs.text:
1. Version Tagging
  2. Use semantic versioning
  3. Follow tag format exactly

  4. Don't skip versions

  5. Changelog Entries

  6. Clear descriptions
  7. Reference PR numbers

  8. Keep format consistent

  9. Release Process

  10. Follow order exactly
  11. Review all changes
  12. Test before release

docs.title: GitHub Actions
docs.text:
• Release Drafter
  • Changelog Updater
  • MkDocs Publisher

docs.title: Repository Settings
docs.text:
• Branch protection
  • Required reviews
  • Status checks

docs.title: Documentation
docs.text:
• Version updates
  • Release notes
  • Changelog links

docs.title: Architecture
docs.text: Our GitHub Actions toolkit follows a layered architecture that promotes reusability, maintainability, and clear separation of concerns.

docs.title: Core Actions
docs.text:
• Atomic, self-contained operations
  • Single responsibility principle
  • Highly reusable
  • Learn more about Core Actions

docs.title: Composite Actions
docs.text:
• Combine core actions
  • Add workflow-specific logic
  • Higher-level operations
  • Learn more about Composite Actions

docs.title: Directory Structure
docs.text:
actions/
├── core/                  # Atomic operations
│   ├── version_calculator/
│   └── version_updater/
└── composite/            # Combined operations
    └── update_changelog/

docs.title: Using Core Actions
docs.text:
steps:
  - name: Calculate Version
    uses: deepworks-net/github.toolkit/actions/core/version_calculator@v1
    with:
      default_version: 'v0.1.0'

docs.title: Using Composite Actions
docs.text:
steps:
  - name: Update Changelog
    uses: deepworks-net/github.toolkit/actions/composite/update_changelog@v1
    with:
      content: ${{ steps.notes.outputs.content }}

docs.title: Docker Configuration
docs.text:
• Standard base image (python:3.9-slim)
  • Consistent dependency management
  • Clear entrypoint configuration

docs.title: Testing
docs.text:
• Comprehensive test workflows
  • Standard test structure
  • Clear naming conventions

docs.title: Documentation
docs.text:
• Complete API documentation
  • Usage examples
  • Error handling guidance

docs.title: Contributing
docs.text:
See our guides for creating:

  • New Core Actions
  • New Composite Actions
  • Action Tests

docs.title: Core
docs.text:
• Version Calculator
  • Version Updater

docs.title: Composite
docs.text: • Update Changelog

docs.title: Changelog Update Action
docs.text: Synchronizes the repository's CHANGELOG.md with content from the draft release, ensuring an "Unreleased" section reflects all recent changes and maintains consistent formatting.

docs.title: Usage
docs.text:
steps:
  - name: Update Changelog
    uses: ./actions/changelog_update
    with:
      github-token: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
      version: ${{ steps.version.outputs.next_version }}

docs.title: Inputs
docs.text: Input Description Required Default github-token GitHub token for API access Yes N/A version Version number to use in the unreleased section Yes N/A

docs.title: Draft Release Content
docs.text:
This action retrieves draft release details via the GitHub API:

  1. Identifies the draft release.
  2. Extracts PR entries.
  3. Strips footer content.

docs.title: Changelog Format
docs.text:
Follows a consistent structure:

# Repository Changelog

## **MM/DD/YYYY - vX.Y.Z Unreleased**
- PR #123: Feature description
- PR #124: Another change

## **[(MM/DD/YYYY) - vX.Y.Z](link-to-release)**
- Previous release content

docs.title: Processing Steps
docs.text:
1. Fetches draft release content.
  2. Updates or creates the "Unreleased" section with:
  3. Current date
  4. Provided version number
  5. Draft release content.
  6. Preserves all previous releases.
  7. Maintains formatting consistency.

docs.title: Environment Setup
docs.text:
• Configures Git in container environments.
  • Installs required tools (e.g., GitHub CLI).
  • Prepares workspace access.

docs.title: Git Operations
docs.text:
Handles:

  • Git configuration.
  • Committing changes.
  • Pushing to a staging branch.

docs.title: Error Handling
docs.text:
Provides clear feedback for:

  1. Missing GitHub token.
  2. API request failures.
  3. File operation issues.
  4. Git command failures.

docs.title: Basic Usage with Version Calculation
docs.text:
jobs:
  update-changelog:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
        with:
          ref: staging
          fetch-depth: 0

      - name: Calculate Version
        uses: ./actions/version_calculation
        id: version

      - name: Update Changelog
        uses: ./actions/changelog_update
        with:
          github-token: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
          version: ${{ steps.version.outputs.next_version }}

docs.title: Manual Version Specification
docs.text:
steps:
  - name: Update Changelog
    uses: ./actions/changelog_update
    with:
      github-token: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
      version: 'v1.0.0'

docs.title: Requirements
docs.text:
1. Repository must include:

    • CHANGELOG.md in the root directory.
    • Draft release for content sourcing.
    • Proper GitHub token permissions.

  2. Environment must have:

    • Git.
    • GitHub CLI.
    • Python 3.9+.

docs.title: action.yml
docs.text:
Defines the action interface:

  • Input parameters.
  • Environment configuration.
  • Docker container setup.

docs.title: Dockerfile
docs.text:
Configures the container with:

  • Python runtime.
  • Git installation.
  • GitHub CLI.
  • Necessary environment setups.

docs.title: update_changelog.py
docs.text:
Implements core logic:

  • Fetches draft release content.
  • Parses and updates the changelog.
  • Manages Git operations.
  • Handles errors.

docs.title: Error Cases
docs.text:
The action accounts for several potential issues:

  1. Missing Draft Release

    • Outputs a clear error message.
    • Returns a non-zero exit code.

  2. GitHub API Issues

    • Validates tokens.
    • Handles request failures or permission errors.

  3. File Operations

    • Detects missing changelog.
    • Handles write permissions or format inconsistencies.

  4. Git Operations

    • Resolves configuration or push errors.
    • Handles permission issues.

docs.title: Future Improvements
docs.text:
Possible enhancements:

  1. Support custom changelog formats.
  2. Handle multiple changelog files.
  3. Categorize release notes.
  4. Automate version bumping.
  5. Customize commit messages.

docs.title: Integration Points
docs.text:
Designed to integrate with:

  • Version calculation actions.
  • Release preparation workflows.
  • GitHub release drafts.
  • Branch protection rules.

docs.title: Contributing
docs.text:
To contribute:

  1. Update the Python script for logic changes.
  2. Test with various changelog states.
  3. Verify formatting consistency.
  4. Submit a PR for review.

docs.title: What are Core Actions?
docs.text: Core actions are atomic, self-contained operations that serve as the fundamental building blocks in our GitHub Actions toolkit. They follow the principle of "do one thing and do it well," making them highly reusable and maintainable.

docs.title: Atomic Operations
docs.text:
• Each core action performs a single, well-defined task
  • Inputs and outputs are clearly specified
  • No hidden dependencies or side effects

docs.title: Self-Contained
docs.text:
• Includes all necessary dependencies
  • Uses standardized Docker configurations
  • Independent of external services (except GitHub)

docs.title: Testable
docs.text:
• Comprehensive test suites
  • Predictable outcomes
  • Clear error conditions

docs.title: Standard Structure
docs.text:
actions/core/<action-name>/
├── main.py                 # Primary action logic
├── action.yml             # Action metadata
├── Dockerfile             # Standardized container config
└── README.md             # Action documentation

docs.title: Docker Configuration Standard
docs.text:
All core actions use a standardized Dockerfile structure:

FROM python:3.9-slim

WORKDIR /action

# Install system dependencies
RUN apt-get update && \
    apt-get install -y git && \
    rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*

# Configure git for workspace
RUN git config --global --add safe.directory /github/workspace

# Install Python dependencies (if needed)
# RUN pip install <dependencies>

# Copy action files
COPY *.py /action/
COPY action.yml /action/

ENTRYPOINT ["python", "/action/main.py"]

docs.title: Version Management
docs.text:
• Version Calculator: Calculates next version from Git history
  • Version Updater: Updates version numbers in files

docs.title: Input Validation
docs.text:
• Validate all inputs before processing
  • Provide clear error messages
  • Fail fast on invalid inputs

docs.title: Output Formatting
docs.text:
• Use GitHub Actions output syntax
  • Document all possible outputs
  • Include error details in outputs

docs.title: Error Handling
docs.text:
• Graceful failure modes
  • Clear error messages
  • Appropriate exit codes

docs.title: Documentation
docs.text:
• Clear usage examples
  • Complete input/output documentation
  • Error handling guidance

docs.title: Testing
docs.text:
Each core action includes a test workflow:

.github/workflows/test.core.action.<name>.yml

Tests validate:

  • Basic functionality
  • Edge cases
  • Error conditions
  • Input variations

docs.title: Overview
docs.text: The Version Calculator is a core (atomic) action that calculates the next version number based on Git tags and commit history. It follows semantic versioning principles while maintaining automatability and predictability.

docs.title: Usage
docs.text:
- name: Calculate Version
  uses: deepworks-net/github.toolkit/actions/core/version_calculator@v1
  with:
    default_version: 'v0.1.0'      # Optional: Default if no tags exist
    version_prefix: 'v'            # Optional: Prefix for version tags
    tag_pattern: 'v*'             # Optional: Pattern to match version tags

docs.title: Inputs
docs.text: Input Description Required Default default_version Default version when no tags exist No v0.1.0 version_prefix Prefix for version tags No v tag_pattern Pattern to match version tags No v*

docs.title: Outputs
docs.text: Output Description next_version The calculated next version current_version Current version (latest tag or default) commit_count Number of commits since last tag

docs.title: Core Files
docs.text:
• main.py: Primary logic for version calculation
  • action.yml: Action metadata and input/output definitions
  • Dockerfile: Standardized container configuration

docs.title: Docker Configuration
docs.text:
FROM python:3.9-slim

WORKDIR /action

# Install system dependencies
RUN apt-get update && \
    apt-get install -y git && \
    rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*

# Configure git for workspace
RUN git config --global --add safe.directory /github/workspace

# Copy action files
COPY *.py /action/
COPY action.yml /action/

ENTRYPOINT ["python", "/action/main.py"]

docs.title: Basic Version Calculation
docs.text:
steps:
  - name: Calculate Version
    id: version
    uses: deepworks-net/github.toolkit/actions/core/version_calculator@v1

  - name: Use Version
    run: echo "Next version will be ${{ steps.version.outputs.next_version }}"

docs.title: Custom Version Prefix
docs.text:
steps:
  - name: Calculate Version
    uses: deepworks-net/github.toolkit/actions/core/version_calculator@v1
    with:
      version_prefix: 'ver'
      tag_pattern: 'ver*'

docs.title: Testing
docs.text:
The action includes comprehensive tests in the test.core.action.version_calculator.yml workflow, which validates:

  • Default version behavior (no tags)
  • Version calculation with existing tags
  • Custom version prefix handling
  • Output completeness

docs.title: Error Handling
docs.text:
The action provides clear error messages for common issues:

  • Invalid version formats
  • Git configuration errors
  • Missing repository access
  • Tag pattern mismatches

docs.title: Overview
docs.text: The Version Updater is a core (atomic) action that updates version numbers across multiple file types (YAML, JSON, and text files). It provides consistent version management while maintaining file-specific formatting.

docs.title: Usage
docs.text:
- name: Update Versions
  uses: deepworks-net/github.toolkit/actions/core/version_updater@v1
  with:
    version: 'v1.0.0'             # Required: Version to set
    files: |  # Required: Files to update (one per line)
          "README.md"
          "CHANGELOG.md"
          "mkdocs.yml"
    strip_v_prefix: true          # Optional: Remove 'v' prefix when updating

docs.title: Inputs
docs.text: Input Description Required Default version Version number to set Yes - files JSON array of file paths to update Yes - strip_v_prefix Remove 'v' prefix when updating files No true

docs.title: Outputs
docs.text: Output Description files JSON array of successfully updated files

docs.title: Core Files
docs.text:
• main.py: Primary logic for version updating
  • action.yml: Action metadata and input/output definitions
  • Dockerfile: Standardized container configuration

docs.title: Docker Configuration
docs.text:
FROM python:3.9-slim

WORKDIR /action

# Install system dependencies
RUN apt-get update && \
    apt-get install -y git && \
    rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*

# Configure git for workspace
RUN git config --global --add safe.directory /github/workspace

# Install Python dependencies
RUN pip install PyYAML

# Copy action files
COPY *.py /action/
COPY action.yml /action/

ENTRYPOINT ["python", "/action/main.py"]

docs.title: YAML Files
docs.text:
• Updates version: fields
  • Preserves file formatting
  • Handles different indentation levels

docs.title: JSON Files
docs.text:
• Updates version key in JSON objects
  • Maintains JSON structure
  • Preserves formatting and indentation

docs.title: Text Files
docs.text:
• Uses regex to find version patterns
  • Updates versions while preserving surrounding text
  • Handles common version formats

docs.title: Update Single File
docs.text:
steps:
  - name: Update Version
    uses: deepworks-net/github.toolkit/actions/core/version_updater@v1
    with:
      version: 'v2.0.0'
      files: |
            "package.json"

docs.title: Update Multiple Files
docs.text:
steps:
  - name: Update Versions
    uses: deepworks-net/github.toolkit/actions/core/version_updater@v1
    with:
      version: 'v2.0.0'
      files: |
            "version.yml"
            "package.json"
            "VERSION"
      strip_v_prefix: false

docs.title: Testing
docs.text:
The action includes comprehensive tests in the test.core.action.version_updater.yml workflow, which validates:

  • YAML file updates
  • JSON file updates
  • Multiple file updates
  • Version prefix handling
  • Error conditions

docs.title: Error Handling
docs.text:
The action provides clear error messages for common issues:

  • File not found
  • Invalid file formats
  • Permission issues
  • Version pattern mismatches

docs.title: GitHub Actions Collection
docs.text: A collection of reusable GitHub Actions workflows for standardizing development processes across repositories.

docs.title: Release Management
docs.text: Two workflows that work together to manage the release process:

docs.title: 1. Prepare Release Branch (prep-release.yml)
docs.text:
Creates a release branch and prepares the changelog for release.

Trigger: Push a tag with prefix prep-v (e.g., prep-v1.0.0)

Actions:

  • Creates a release branch
  • Moves Unreleased changelog items to a new version section
  • Removes the Unreleased section for release
  • Creates a PR for review

Usage:

git tag prep-v1.0.0
git push origin prep-v1.0.0

docs.title: 2. Update Changelog (update-changelog.yml)
docs.text:
Automatically updates the changelog when PRs are merged to develop.

Trigger: PR merged to develop branch

Actions:

  • Adds PR to Unreleased section of changelog
  • Creates Unreleased section if it doesn't exist
  • Maintains changelog formatting

Usage: Automatic - no manual steps required. PR merges to develop trigger the workflow.

docs.title: Changelog Format
docs.text:
The workflows maintain the following changelog format:

# Repository Changelog
*Note: the changes in this log are automatically generated and commited via github actions, modify only if you know what you are doing!*

## **MM/DD/YYYY - Unreleased**
- PR #{number}: {title}

## **[(MM/DD/YYYY) - {version}](https://github.com/{org}/{repo}/releases/tag/{version})**
- PR #{number}: {title}

docs.title: Setup Instructions
docs.text:
1. Copy the desired workflow files to your repository's .github/workflows/ directory
  2. No additional configuration needed - workflows use repository context for variables

docs.title: Requirements
docs.text:
• GitHub repository with develop branch
  • Permissions to push tags and create PRs
  • Changelog.md file in repository root (will be created if missing)

docs.title: Contributing
docs.text:
1. Create a feature branch off develop
  2. Make your changes
  3. Create a PR to develop
  4. Changelog will be automatically updated upon merge

docs.title: Support
docs.text:
For issues, questions, or contributions:

  1. Open an issue in this repository
  2. Include workflow name and description of need

docs.title: License
docs.text: MIT License - See LICENSE.md file for details

docs.title: GitHub Actions Collection
docs.text: A collection of reusable GitHub Actions workflows for standardizing development processes across repositories.

docs.title: Release Management
docs.text: Two workflows that work together to manage the release process:

docs.title: 1. Prepare Release Branch (prep-release.yml)
docs.text:
Creates a release branch and prepares the changelog for release.

Trigger: Push a tag with prefix prep-v (e.g., prep-v1.0.0)

Actions:

  • Creates a release branch
  • Moves Unreleased changelog items to a new version section
  • Removes the Unreleased section for release
  • Creates a PR for review

Usage:

git tag prep-v1.0.0

Trigger: PR merged to develop branch

Actions:

  • Adds PR to Unreleased section of changelog

docs.title: Create Release Tag Workflow
docs.text: This workflow is part of the release automation process. It creates and pushes version tags when release PRs are merged to the main branch, triggering the final release publication.

docs.title: Workflow File
docs.text: .github/workflows/create-tag.yml

docs.title: Trigger
docs.text:
The workflow triggers when pull requests targeting the main branch are closed:

on:
  pull_request:
    branches:
      - main
    types: [closed]

docs.title: Conditions
docs.text:
The workflow only runs when:

  1. The PR was merged (not just closed)
  2. The source branch name starts with release/v
if: github.event.pull_request.merged == true && startsWith(github.event.pull_request.head.ref, 'release/v')

docs.title: Process
docs.text:
1. Checks out the repository
  2. Extracts version number from the release branch name
  3. Creates a git tag with that version
  4. Pushes the tag to the repository

docs.title: Integration Points
docs.text:
This workflow is part of the release process chain:

  1. Prepare Release workflow creates release branch (release/vX.Y.Z)
  2. PR is created targeting main branch
  3. PR is reviewed and merged
  4. Create Tag workflow (this one) creates version tag
  5. Release Drafter workflow publishes final release

docs.title: Version Tag Format
docs.text:
Tags created by this workflow match the release branch name:

  • Branch: release/v1.0.34
  • Creates tag: v1.0.34

docs.title: Prerequisites
docs.text:
1. Source branch must:
  2. Start with release/v
  3. Contain valid version number

  4. Be merged to main branch

  5. Repository settings:

  6. Allow workflows to create tags
  7. Proper access permissions set

docs.title: Permissions
docs.text:
The workflow requires:

  • Read access to repository
  • Write access for tags
  • Uses GITHUB_TOKEN with default permissions

docs.title: Error Handling
docs.text:
The workflow will fail if:

  • PR is not actually merged
  • Branch name doesn't match pattern
  • Tag already exists
  • Insufficient permissions

docs.title: Usage Example
docs.text:
1. Release PR is created:
release/v1.0.34 -> main

  1. PR is merged to main

  2. Workflow automatically:

git tag v1.0.34
git push origin v1.0.34

docs.title: Troubleshooting
docs.text:
Common issues and solutions:

  1. Tag Creation Fails
  2. Check if tag already exists
  3. Verify branch name format

  4. Check workflow permissions

  5. Workflow Doesn't Trigger

  6. Verify PR target is main branch
  7. Check branch name starts with release/v

  8. Ensure PR was merged, not just closed

  9. Push Fails

  10. Check repository permissions
  11. Verify token access
  12. Review workflow logs

docs.title: Related Workflows
docs.text:
• Prepare Release
  • Release Drafter
  • Update Changelog

docs.title: Next Steps
docs.text:
After this workflow runs:

  1. Version tag is created
  2. Release Drafter workflow triggers
  3. Final release is published

docs.title: Contributing
docs.text:
To modify this workflow:

  1. Fork the repository
  2. Edit .github/workflows/create-tag.yml
  3. Test with a release branch
  4. Submit a pull request

docs.title: MkDocs GitHub Pages Workflow
docs.text: This reusable workflow automates the process of building and deploying MkDocs documentation to GitHub Pages. It can be used in any repository that uses MkDocs for documentation.

docs.title: Workflow File
docs.text: .github/workflows/mkdocs-gh-pages.yml

docs.title: Basic Usage
docs.text:
To use this workflow in your repository, create a workflow file with the following content:

name: Deploy Documentation
on:
  push:
    branches:
      - main

jobs:
  deploy-docs:
    uses: deepworks-net/github.actions/.github/workflows/mkdocs-gh-pages.yml@main

docs.title: Advanced Usage
docs.text:
You can customize the workflow behavior using input parameters:

name: Deploy Documentation
on:
  push:
    branches:
      - main

jobs:
  deploy-docs:
    uses: deepworks-net/github.actions/.github/workflows/mkdocs-gh-pages.yml@main
    with:
      python-version: '3.11'
      requirements-file: 'requirements.txt'
      readme-source: 'README.md'
      readme-destination: 'docs/repo/inc/README.md'
      changelog-source: 'CHANGELOG'
      changelog-destination: 'docs/repo/inc/CHANGELOG.md'
      license-source: 'LICENSE'
      license-destination: 'docs/repo/inc/LICENSE.md'

docs.title: Input Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Description Required Default python-version Python version to use for the build No 3.x requirements-file Path to the requirements.txt file No requirements.txt readme-source Source path for README No README.md readme-destination Destination path for README No docs/repo/inc/README.md changelog-source Source path for CHANGELOG No CHANGELOG changelog-destination Destination path for CHANGELOG No docs/repo/inc/CHANGELOG.md license-source Source path for LICENSE No LICENSE license-destination Destination path for LICENSE No docs/repo/inc/LICENSE.md

docs.title: Workflow Details
docs.text:
The workflow performs the following steps:

  1. Checks out the repository with submodules
  2. Sets up Python environment
  3. Installs dependencies from requirements.txt
  4. Copies README.md (if it exists) to docs/repo/inc directory
  5. Copies CHANGELOG (if it exists) to docs/repo/inc directory
  6. Copies LICENSE (if it exists) to docs/repo/inc directory
  7. Builds and deploys MkDocs to GitHub Pages

docs.title: Requirements
docs.text:
To use this workflow, your repository needs:

  1. MkDocs configuration file (mkdocs.yml)
  2. Python dependencies file (requirements.txt) including MkDocs and any required theme/plugins
  3. GitHub Pages enabled in repository settings
  4. Proper permissions for GitHub Actions

docs.title: Example requirements.txt
docs.text:
mkdocs>=1.6.1
mkdocs-material>=9.5.49

docs.title: Common Issues
docs.text:
1. Build Fails: Ensure all required dependencies are listed in requirements.txt
  2. Deploy Fails: Check GitHub Pages settings in repository
  3. Missing README: Verify README.md exists at specified source path

docs.title: Workflow Logs
docs.text:
To check workflow execution logs:

  1. Go to Actions tab in your repository
  2. Click on the workflow run
  3. Expand the deploy job to see detailed logs

docs.title: Security Considerations
docs.text:
• The workflow runs with repository contents and GitHub token permissions
  • No additional secrets are required for basic usage
  • Ensure sensitive information is not exposed in documentation

docs.title: Contributing
docs.text: Found a bug or have a suggestion? Please open an issue in the deepworks-net/github.actions repository.

docs.title: Prepare Release Workflow
docs.text: This page documents the Prepare Release workflow located in the .github/workflows/prepare-release.yml file. This workflow automates the process of preparing a release branch and creating a pull request for review.

docs.title: Overview
docs.text:
The Prepare Release workflow is triggered manually by tagging a commit with the tag:

prep

It automates the following steps:

  • Creates a release branch.
  • Moves items from the "Unreleased" section of the changelog to a new versioned section.
  • Removes the "Unreleased" section to finalize the changelog for release.
  • Creates a pull request (PR) for the release branch.

docs.title: Trigger
docs.text:
To activate this workflow, push a tag with the prefix prep-v. For example:

git tag prep-v1.0.0
git push origin prep-v1.0.0

docs.title: 1. Check Out Repository
docs.text: The workflow checks out the repository code to the GitHub Actions runner to perform modifications.

docs.title: 2. Create Release Branch
docs.text: A new branch named release/{version} is created. The version is extracted from the tag name (e.g., prep-v1.0.0 creates release/1.0.0).

docs.title: 3. Update Changelog
docs.text:
The workflow:

  • Moves all changelog items under the "Unreleased" section to a new version section (e.g., ## [1.0.0]).
  • Adds a date to the new section (based on the workflow run date).
  • Removes the "Unreleased" section.

docs.title: 4. Commit Changes
docs.text:
Commits the updated changelog to the new release branch with a message like:

chore: prepare release 1.0.0

docs.title: 5. Create Pull Request
docs.text:
Creates a PR from the release branch to the main branch. The PR title includes the version, such as:

makefile

Copy code

Release: 1.0.0

docs.title: Repository Setup
docs.text:
1. A develop branch is required for ongoing development.
  2. A Changelog.md file must exist in the repository root. If not present, the workflow will create one.

docs.title: Permissions
docs.text:
• Write access to create branches and push changes.
  • Access to create and manage PRs.

docs.title: Usage Example
docs.text:
1. Ensure your changelog has an "Unreleased" section with pending changes.
  2. Tag the repository with a release preparation version:

bash

Copy code

git tag prep-v2.1.0 git push origin prep-v2.1.0

  1. The workflow will:
    • Create the branch release/2.1.0.
    • Update the changelog.
    • Open a PR for review and approval.

docs.title: Outputs
docs.text:
• Release Branch: release/{version} (e.g., release/2.1.0).
  • Updated Changelog: Moves changes to the new version section with a date.
  • Pull Request: A PR for merging the release branch into main.

docs.title: Notes
docs.text:
• If the changelog format deviates from the expected structure, the workflow may fail.
  • Manual edits to the changelog after workflow execution are discouraged to maintain consistency.

docs.title: Workflow Fails
docs.text:
1. Check the workflow logs for details.
  2. Verify the tag format (prep-v{version}) and changelog structure.

docs.title: Missing Changelog
docs.text: If the Changelog.md file is missing, the workflow will create one with a default template.

docs.title: Contributing
docs.text:
For improvements or fixes to this workflow, follow these steps:

  1. Fork the repository.
  2. Create a branch from develop.
  3. Submit a PR with a detailed description of your changes.

docs.title: License
docs.text:
This workflow is part of the Deepworks project and is licensed under the MIT License.

For more details, visit the repository: deepworks-net/github.actions

docs.title: Release Drafter Workflow
docs.text: This workflow automates the creation and management of GitHub Releases. It maintains a draft release that updates automatically with new changes. It also allows other workflows to trigger release operations via a workflow call.

docs.title: Workflow File
docs.text: .github/workflows/release-drafter.yml

docs.title: Triggers
docs.text:
The workflow responds to two types of events:

  1. Push to the develop branch:

    • Updates the draft release.
    • Calculates the next version using a centralized version calculation.
    • Updates release notes dynamically.

  2. Workflow call:

    • Allows other workflows to trigger release operations.
    • Supports custom tag names and options.

docs.title: Version Calculation
docs.text:
The workflow calculates the next version number using the version_calculation Action, based on:

  • The latest version tag (vX.Y.Z format).
  • The number of commits since the last tag. This ensures consistent and centralized versioning logic across workflows.

Example:

  • Latest version: v1.0.16
  • 3 new commits.
  • Next calculated version: v1.0.19.

docs.title: 1. Update Release Draft
docs.text:
Triggered when changes are pushed to the develop branch:

jobs:
  update_release_draft:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    if: github.ref == 'refs/heads/develop'

Steps:

  1. Checkout Repository:
    • Ensures the develop branch is checked out.
    • Fetches the full Git history for accurate version calculations.
  2. Debugging Steps:
    • Confirms the repository structure and Action files are present.
  3. Calculate Next Version:
    • Uses the centralized version_calculation Action.
  4. Set Output for Version:
    • Saves the calculated version as a workflow output for future steps.
  5. Draft Release:
    • Updates the draft release with the calculated version and release notes.

docs.title: 2. Publish Release (Conditional)
docs.text:
This job is configured to publish a final release if the workflow is triggered with a refs/tags/v* reference. It is conditional on such an event being passed to the workflow:

jobs:
  publish_release:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    if: startsWith(github.ref, 'refs/tags/v')

Steps:

  1. Checkout Repository:
    • Ensures the correct repository and tag are available.
  2. Publish Final Release:
    • Publishes the release using the release-drafter/release-drafter@v6 Action.
    • Uses the provided tag to finalize release notes.

docs.title: Input Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Description Required Default tag-name Release tag name No Current tag draft Create as draft No false

docs.title: Secrets
docs.text: Secret Description Required Default token GitHub token No GITHUB_TOKEN

docs.title: Draft Release
docs.text:
## Draft Release v1.0.X

[Automatically populated release notes]

See the [Changelog](https://github.com/${{ github.repository }}/blob/main/CHANGELOG.md) for more details.

docs.title: Integration
docs.text:
This workflow integrates with:

  • Centralized version calculation logic.
  • Draft release management.
  • Optional release publishing.

docs.title: Permissions
docs.text:
Required permissions:

permissions:
  contents: write

docs.title: As Part of Release Process
docs.text:
1. Push changes to the develop branch:
    • Updates the draft release.
    • Dynamically calculates the next version.
    • Updates release notes.

docs.title: Called from Another Workflow
docs.text:
jobs:
  release:
    uses: deepworks-net/github.actions/.github/workflows/release-drafter.yml@main
    with:
      tag-name: 'v1.0.0'
      draft: true

docs.title: Common Issues and Solutions
docs.text:
1. Version Calculation Fails:

    • Ensure the repository has at least one version tag.
    • Verify the version_calculation Action is properly configured.

  2. Draft Not Updating:

    • Ensure the branch name matches develop.
    • Verify the workflow has write permissions.
    • Check the release-drafter configuration.

docs.title: Related Documentation
docs.text:
• release-drafter action
  • Semantic Versioning
  • GitHub Releases

docs.title: Update Changelog Workflow
docs.text: This workflow automatically updates the repository's CHANGELOG.md file when pull requests are merged into the develop branch.

docs.title: Workflow File
docs.text: .github/workflows/update-changelog.yml

docs.title: Trigger
docs.text:
The workflow is triggered when a pull request targeting the develop branch is closed:

on:
  pull_request:
    branches:
      - develop
    types: [closed]

The workflow will only run if the pull request was actually merged, not just closed:

jobs:
  update-changelog:
    if: github.event.pull_request.merged == true

docs.title: Changelog Format
docs.text:
The workflow expects and maintains a changelog in the following format:

## **MM/DD/YYYY - Unreleased**
- PR #123: Description of change
- PR #124: Another change

## **MM/DD/YYYY - v1.0.0**
- Previous release changes...

docs.title: Process
docs.text:
The workflow performs the following steps:

  1. Checks out the repository's develop branch
  2. Processes the CHANGELOG.md file:
    • If an "Unreleased" section exists, adds the new PR entry to it
    • If no "Unreleased" section exists, creates one with today's date
  3. Commits and pushes the updated changelog

docs.title: Entry Format
docs.text:
Each changelog entry follows the format:

- PR #{number}: {pull request title}

docs.title: Usage
docs.text: The workflow runs automatically when PRs are merged. No manual intervention is required.

docs.title: Prerequisites
docs.text:
1. Repository must have a CHANGELOG.md file in the root directory
  2. The file should follow the expected format
  3. PRs should be merged into the develop branch

docs.title: Pull Request Requirements
docs.text:
• PRs must target the develop branch
  • PR title should be descriptive as it will be used in the changelog
  • PR must be merged (not just closed)

docs.title: PR Title Examples
docs.text:
Good PR titles that make meaningful changelog entries:

Add user authentication system
Fix memory leak in data processing
Update dependencies to latest versions

Poor PR titles to avoid:

Fix bug
Update code
WIP: Changes

docs.title: Changelog Entry Examples
docs.text:
The workflow will create entries like:

## **01/02/2025 - Unreleased**
- PR #123: Add user authentication system
- PR #124: Fix memory leak in data processing
- PR #125: Update dependencies to latest versions

docs.title: Permissions
docs.text:
The workflow requires:

  • Read access to pull request metadata
  • Write access to the repository (for pushing changes)

It uses the GITHUB_TOKEN secret which is automatically provided by GitHub Actions.

docs.title: Error Handling
docs.text:
The workflow includes several safeguards:

  1. Only runs on merged PRs
  2. Creates "Unreleased" section if missing
  3. Checks for changes before committing
  4. Uses [skip ci] in commit message to prevent CI loops

docs.title: Troubleshooting
docs.text:
Common issues and solutions:

  1. Changelog Not Updated

    • Verify PR was actually merged
    • Check if PR targeted develop branch
    • Verify CHANGELOG.md exists in root directory

  2. Push Failed

    • Check if workflow has write permissions
    • Verify branch protections allow GitHub Actions to push

  3. Format Issues

    • Ensure CHANGELOG.md follows expected format
    • Check for manual modifications that might break format

docs.title: Contributing
docs.text:
To modify this workflow:

  1. Fork the repository
  2. Edit .github/workflows/update-changelog.yml
  3. Test changes by merging PRs to your fork
  4. Submit a pull request

docs.title: Related Documentation
docs.text:
• GitHub Actions documentation
  • Keep a Changelog
  • Git documentation

docs.title: Overview
docs.text: The Version Calculator Workflow is a reusable GitHub workflow designed to calculate the next version of a repository based on the latest Git tag and commit history. It integrates seamlessly into external repositories for dynamic versioning.

docs.title: Key Features
docs.text:
• Dynamically calculates the next version.
  • Provides the current version and commit count since the last tag.
  • Designed for external repositories to reuse without modifying internal workflows.

docs.title: 1. default_version (Optional)
docs.text:
• Description: Default version to use when no tags exist.
  • Type: String
  • Default: v0.1.0

docs.title: 2. version_prefix (Optional)
docs.text:
• Description: Prefix for version tags (e.g., v in v1.0.0).
  • Type: String
  • Default: v

docs.title: 3. tag_pattern (Optional)
docs.text:
• Description: Pattern to match version tags.
  • Type: String
  • Default: v*

docs.title: 1. next_version
docs.text: • Description: The calculated next version.

docs.title: 2. current_version
docs.text: • Description: The current version (latest tag or default).

docs.title: 3. commit_count
docs.text: • Description: The number of commits since the last tag.

docs.title: Expected Behavior
docs.text:
When the Version Calculator Workflow is used:

  1. Initial Run with No Tags:

    • If no tags exist in the repository, the workflow uses the default_version input (defaulting to v0.1.0).
    • Outputs:
      • current_version: v0.1.0
      • next_version: v0.1.0
      • commit_count: Total commits in the repository.

  2. Run with Existing Tags:

    • The workflow identifies the latest tag matching the tag_pattern.
    • It calculates the number of commits since the latest tag and increments the patch version accordingly.
    • Outputs:
      • current_version: Latest tag.
      • next_version: Incremented patch version.
      • commit_count: Commits since the latest tag.

  3. Edge Cases:

    • If the latest tag does not match the tag_pattern, the workflow defaults to default_version behavior.
    • If there are no new commits since the latest tag, the next_version will match the current_version.

docs.title: Workflow Call
docs.text:
To use the Version Calculator Workflow in an external repository:

name: Calculate Version

on:
  workflow_dispatch:  # Allows manual triggering

jobs:
  calculate-version:
    uses: your-username/github.actions/.github/workflows/version_calculator.yml@v1
    with:
      default_version: "v0.1.0"
      version_prefix: "v"
      tag_pattern: "v*"

    outputs:
      next_version: ${{ jobs.calculate-version.outputs.next_version }}
      current_version: ${{ jobs.calculate-version.outputs.current_version }}
      commit_count: ${{ jobs.calculate-version.outputs.commit_count }}

docs.title: Using Outputs in Subsequent Steps
docs.text:
Once the workflow is invoked, its outputs can be used in downstream jobs or steps:

      - name: Use Calculated Version
        run: |
          echo "Next version: ${{ jobs.calculate-version.outputs.next_version }}"
          echo "Current version: ${{ jobs.calculate-version.outputs.current_version }}"
          echo "Commits since last tag: ${{ jobs.calculate-version.outputs.commit_count }}"

docs.title: Debugging Tips
docs.text:
1. Ensure Proper Tags Exist: Verify that version tags in the repository match the specified tag_pattern.
  2. Fetch Entire History: Set fetch-depth: 0 in the actions/checkout step to include all tags and commits.
  3. Validate Inputs: Double-check that input parameters match the repository's versioning scheme.

docs.title: 1. version_calculator.yml
docs.text: Defines the reusable workflow with input and output specifications.

docs.title: 2. version_calculator.py
docs.text: The Python script used for version calculation (referenced within the action).

docs.title: Workflow Highlights
docs.text:
on:
  workflow_dispatch:  # Allows manual triggering
  workflow_call:
    inputs:
      default_version:
        type: string
        required: false
        default: 'v0.1.0'
      version_prefix:
        type: string
        required: false
        default: 'v'
      tag_pattern:
        type: string
        required: false
        default: 'v*'
    outputs:
      next_version:
        description: "Calculated next version"
        value: ${{ jobs.calculate-version.outputs.next_version }}
      current_version:
        description: "Current version"
        value: ${{ jobs.calculate-version.outputs.current_version }}
      commit_count:
        description: "Commit count since last tag"
        value: ${{ jobs.calculate-version.outputs.commit_count }}

docs.title: Workflow Outputs
docs.text:
• next_version: Computed based on commits since the last tag.
  • current_version: The latest tag or default version.
  • commit_count: Number of commits since the last tag.

docs.title: Overview
docs.text: The Version Updater Workflow is a reusable GitHub workflow designed to update version numbers in specified configuration files. It integrates seamlessly into external repositories for consistent version management across various file formats.

docs.title: Key Features
docs.text:
• Dynamically updates version numbers in YAML, JSON, and text files.
  • Supports version standardization by removing the v prefix if specified.
  • Allows external repositories to reuse the workflow by providing inputs for version control.

docs.title: 1. version (Required)
docs.text:
• Description: The version number to set.
  • Type: String
  • Required: Yes

docs.title: 2. files (Required)
docs.text:
• Description: List of file paths to update. Accepts a JSON array of file paths.
  • Type: String
  • Required: Yes

docs.title: 3. strip_v_prefix (Optional)
docs.text:
• Description: Whether to remove the v prefix from the version number.
  • Type: Boolean
  • Default: true

docs.title: Outputs
docs.text: The Version Updater Workflow does not produce explicit outputs but relies on logs and the updated files for verification and validation.

docs.title: Expected Behavior
docs.text:
When the Version Updater Workflow is used:

  1. With Provided Inputs:

    • The workflow fetches the repository's code and iterates through the files specified in the files input.
    • It updates the version field in each file according to the file type.

  2. File Types and Behavior:

    • YAML Files: Updates version: fields.
    • JSON Files: Updates the version key in JSON objects.
    • Generic Text Files: Uses regex to locate and replace version definitions.

  3. Edge Cases:

    • If a file does not contain a recognizable version field, a warning is logged, and the workflow continues.
    • Missing or invalid files are skipped with a warning in the logs.

docs.title: Workflow Call
docs.text:
To use the Version Updater Workflow in an external repository:

name: Update Version

on:
  workflow_dispatch:  # Allows manual triggering

jobs:
  update-version:
    uses: your-username/github.actions/.github/workflows/version_updater.yml@v1
    with:
      version: "v2.0.1"
      files: '["config.yml", "package.json", "README.md"]'
      strip_v_prefix: false

docs.title: Logs for Validation
docs.text:
The workflow provides logs for each file it updates, making it easy to verify changes:

Updated version in config.yml to v2.0.1
Updated version in package.json to v2.0.1
Warning: No version field found in README.md

docs.title: Debugging Tips
docs.text:
1. Validate Inputs: Ensure the version and files inputs are correctly specified.
  2. Check File Accessibility: Confirm that all file paths provided are valid and accessible.
  3. Review Logs: The workflow logs detailed information about updates and skips, which can help identify issues.

docs.title: 1. version_updater.yml
docs.text: Defines the reusable workflow with input specifications and steps to execute the Version Updater action.

docs.title: 2. version_updater.py
docs.text: The Python script used by the action to update files.

docs.title: Workflow Metadata
docs.text:
on:
  workflow_dispatch:  # Allows manual triggering
  workflow_call:
    inputs:
      version:
        required: true
        type: string
      files:
        required: true
        type: string
      strip_v_prefix:
        required: false
        type: boolean
        default: true

jobs:
  update-version:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - name: Checkout code
        uses: actions/checkout@v4
        with:
          repository: ${{ github.repository }}
          ref: main
          fetch-depth: 0

      - name: Update The Version
        id: update-version
        uses: deepworks-net/github.toolkit/actions/version_updater@v1
        with:
          version: ${{ inputs.version }}
          files: ${{ inputs.files }}
          strip_v_prefix: ${{ inputs.strip_v_prefix }}

docs.title: Steps in Workflow
docs.text:
1. Checkout Code: Ensures the repository is available for modification.
  2. Run Version Updater: Invokes the Version Updater action with provided inputs.
